sections.title: Expressing the future: will/won't and be going to

content.subtitle: Will/Won't
items: Facts about the future: The website will come online next week.
Predictions not based on present evidence: In the future, everyone will have their own flying car.
Decisions made at the moment of speaking: I've decided! I won't get a new DVD player just yet.
Offers and suggestions: I'll help you with your physics homework, if you like.
Requests: Will you help me with my physics homework?
Most first conditional sentences: If we get a computer, we'll be able to surf the Internet.

content.subtitle: Be going to
items: Plans and intentions: I'm going to be a famous doctor one day!
Predictions based on present evidence: It sounds like the plane's going to take off in a few minutes.

content.subtitle: Note on Shall
items: With offers and suggestions in the question form, we use shall with I and we: Shall I help you with your physics homework?

content.subtitle: Use
items: Arrangements made before the moment of speaking: Are they installing the new computers next week?

content.subtitle: Use
items: Timetables, arrangements and fixed events: The bus to the science museum leaves at 8 o'clock tomorrow morning.
After if in first conditional and zero conditional sentences: If technology continues to advance so quickly, what will life be like in a hundred years?
After certain time expressions: We'll find out as soon as we get to the lab.

content.subtitle: Form
items: will/won't + have + past participle

content.subtitle: Use
items: Actions which are completed some time between now and a point in the future: I'll have finished my chemistry homework by the time you come home.

content.subtitle: Form
items: will/won't + be + -ing form

content.subtitle: Use
items: Actions in progress at a point in the future: This time next week, I'll be taking my biology exam.
Habits or repeated actions at a point in the future: In the future, we'll all be flying around using jet-packs.

content.subtitle: Form
items: will/won't + have + been + -ing form

content.subtitle: Use
items: Actions in progress up to a point in the future: At seven o'clock, I'll have been doing my chemistry homework for three hours!

content.subtitle: Key time words
items: when: It'll be wonderful when scientists find / have found a cure for cancer.
as soon as: Let me know as soon as your new computer arrives / has arrived.
before: It'll be several years before we send / we've sent a manned mission to Mars.
after: Let's go for a pizza after we go / have been to the natural history museum.
until/till: The rocket won't be launched until they do / have done a final check.
while: Think of me while you travel / are travelling to the Moon!
once: We'll stop for petrol once we pass / we've passed Cambridge.

content.subtitle: Time
items: (from) Monday to Friday, on Monday, on my birthday/Easter Sunday/etc, in July, on September 20th, in 2008, in (the) summer, at three o'clock, in/for an hour, at the moment, in the morning/afternoon/evening, at night, in/on time, just in time for, in the beginning/end, at the beginning/end of, at the age of, at the weekend, next/last week

content.subtitle: Place
items: turn right at a place, sit on sth, go in(to) a building, wait in(side) a building, arrive in London/Greece, arrive at the stadium, in/on/at the corner (of), come/go/walk/etc to a place, next to/beside/by the building, at/on the front/back of, in front of/behind the station, go out of a building, go towards the station, between the two buildings, opposite the station

content.subtitle: American English notes
items: US speakers do not always use 'on' before days of the week: We've got a biology test Monday.
US speakers often say 'Monday through Friday' instead of 'from Monday to Friday'.

sections.title: A: Will vs. Be going to
sections.instructions: Click the correct option(s). Sometimes both are correct.

items.sentence: The universe **will/be going to** continue to expand for billions of years.
Look out! You **will/be going to** hit the car in front!
I **will/be going to** do the washing-up tonight, if you like.
Do you think that, in the future, people **will/be going to** live to be hundreds of years old?
I **will/be going to** carry some of those bags for you, if you like.
I know! I **will/be going to** have a barbecue on my birthday!
You **will/be going to** hold this door open for me for a minute? Thanks a lot!
We've decided we **will/be going to** try that new restaurant after the play tomorrow night.
We **will/be going to** fly to Jamaica in the summer.
We **will/be going to** invite Tony and Tim round tonight.
You **will/be going to** go to Jason's party next Saturday?

sections.title: B: Circle the correct word or phrase
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase. If both options are correct, circle both.

items.sentence: **We aren't / We're not** going to miss the train, are we?
I think **I'm going to do / I'm doing** really badly in the English test tomorrow.
Josh **is going to sing / is singing** a song in the school talent contest next week.
I'm **going to buy / buying** a big yacht if I can when I'm older.
Sports Day is **going to be / being** held on the last day of term.
**Does / Will** the concert on Thursday last more than two hours?
**Are they going to broadcast / Will they broadcast** the music awards live tomorrow night?
items.answer: Are they going to broadcast/Will they broadcast

items.sentence: Do they **broadcast / Are they broadcasting** the music awards live tomorrow night?
According to the timetable, the train for Oxford **leaves / is leaving** at 10.15.
**Does the restaurant open / Is the restaurant opening** next Sunday?
items.answer: Does the restaurant open/Is the restaurant opening

sections.title: C: Future forms (gap fill)
sections.instructions: Write a word or short phrase in each gap.

items.sentence: At the end of this month, I'll (1) ...... working on my physics project for about six weeks.
I'll (2) ...... spending the whole day in the physics lab next Saturday doing experiments.
I (3) ...... playing football the weekend after either because I've got to go back to the lab then, too.
I guess I'll (4) ...... doing lots of similar projects when I'm at university, so it's good practice.
when I leave university in about four years, I will (5) ...... studying physics for over thirteen years.

sections.title: D: Future perfect and continuous
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the verb in brackets.

items.sentence: I ...... (cook) dinner by the time you get home.
In a few minutes, I ...... (wait) here for Craig for over two hours.
We'll be halfway through the sponsored swim in one hour so we ...... (swim) for forty-eight hours non-stop by then.
They ...... (not / finish) painting the house by the time we get back from holiday.
If she's still on the phone at eight o'clock, Jan ...... (talk) to Melissa for over two hours.
At six o'clock this evening, we ...... (not / climb) for five hours but for seven hours!
This time next month, you ...... (probably / pass) your driving test!
Will you ...... (do) all your homework by bedtime?
Tomorrow, Elaine ...... (work) on the project for ten days.
We ...... (probably / not / leave) by the time you get home.

sections.title: E: Time clauses (transformation)
sections.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
We'll get home and then we'll have something to eat. **got**
We'll have something to eat ...... home.

I'll finish marking the exams and then I'll tell you your results. **soon**
I'll tell you your results ...... marking the exams.

You'll be travelling across the Sahara next week so remember to wear lots of sunscreen! **while**
Remember to wear lots of sunscreen ...... across the Sahara next week!

They'll show Titan at the cinema and then they'll release the DVD. **before**
They won't release the DVD ...... at the cinema.

My science exam starts at ten tomorrow morning. **taking**
At ten past ten tomorrow ...... my science exam.

Let's watch the space documentary before we have supper. **watched**
Let's have supper ...... the space documentary.

I'll send out all the invitations before lunchtime. **have**
By lunchtime, ...... all the invitations.

Do some revision and then I'll give you a test. **until**
I won't give you a test ...... some revision.

When you visit us, we'll have moved into our new house. **by**
We'll have moved into our new house ...... you visit us.

We'll get some more information and then we'll make a decision. **once**
Let's make a decision ...... got some more information.

sections.title: G: Prepositions of time
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: I'll be 100 years old ...... the year 2095!
Sasha's not going to have a party ...... her birthday this year.
See you ...... August!
See you ...... Tuesday!
See you ...... eight o'clock!
See you ...... the morning!
See you ...... a couple of hours!
It's difficult to sleep ...... night ...... the summer because of the heat.
My grandfather left home ...... the age of fourteen!
We got there just ...... time for the movie.
He's never late, and he's never early; he always arrives right ...... time.
My birthday's ...... April 1st.

sections.title: H: Prepositions of place (error correction)
sections.instructions: Each of the words in bold is incorrect. Rewrite them correctly.

items.sentence: My Australian cousins are coming **in** Greece next month!
Turn left **on** the post office, then go straight on.
There weren't any chairs, so we had to sit **at** the floor.
The TV's **on** the corner of the room.
There's a photo of the author **at** the back cover of the book.
We should arrive **at** Paris at six in the morning.
Could you go out **from** the room for a moment, please?
Walk **in** the station, but turn left a couple of blocks before you get there.
I'll meet you **in** the corner of your street.
There should be a broom **on** the back of the cupboard, somewhere.
They should arrive **in** the airport in about an hour.
The CD should be next **from** the CD player.

sections.title: I: Cloze
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Ages, eras and wars will always be defined (1) ...... they are over, or at least well after they (2) ...... started.
(3) ...... the year 1914, for example, no one said: 'Tomorrow I'm going (4) ...... go and fight in the First World War.'
Similarly, no one ever said: 'Next year (5) ...... be the start of the Industrial Revolution.'
(6) ...... the time we are old, we will all (7) ...... experienced enormous technological advances.
We might even (8) ...... walking round with computer chips implanted in our bodies, or perhaps computer chip technology will have (9) ...... replaced by even more advanced technology.
There's talk (10) ...... the moment that human skin itself might make an excellent electronic circuit board.
And nobody knows what the era we will live in (11) ...... the near future will be called by future historians.

If we already live in the Computer Age or the Information Age as some people suggest, the present-day era (12) ...... be referred to by future historians, then who knows what era we're just (13) ...... the beginning of right now?
